source: FINDINGS.md F-002 (discovered during Phase 3 env precheck)
title: Multiple Python scripts in repo crash on default Windows cp1252 stdout when printing ✓/✗/box-drawing chars
component: testing/test_requirements.py, testing/test_runner.py, pi_agent.py:_health_check, pi_agent.py:_get_system_prompt (mode block uses ═ box characters)
what_failed: On Windows Python 3.13 with default stdout encoding (cp1252), `print('✓ ...')` raises UnicodeEncodeError. Reproduced during Phase 3 env pre-check on `python testing/test_requirements.py`. The env-var checking logic itself works; only the printout crashes.
where_failed: testing/test_requirements.py:24 (the f-string with ✓), testing/test_runner.py (multiple ✓/✗), pi_agent.py:653 (_health_check), pi_agent.py:94-100 and 103-109 (mode-block box characters via \u2550).
why_likely: Author developed in a UTF-8-capable terminal (or set PYTHONIOENCODING globally). The repo runs fine for that author but breaks on a fresh Windows shell. Latest production session bfe9f64b was logged successfully, so Ash's working setup has UTF-8 stdout — but a new contributor or a CI runner would not.
reproduction: On Windows, fresh shell, no PYTHONIOENCODING set: `python testing/test_requirements.py` raises UnicodeEncodeError. Workaround: `$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'; python testing/test_requirements.py` works.
expected: Tests and pi_agent.py run without env-var workaround on default Windows shell.
suggested_fix: Two cheap options: (a) replace ✓/✗ with [OK]/[FAIL] in test files; replace box characters with ASCII = signs in pi_agent.py. Loses some pretty-printing on Linux but works everywhere. (b) Set sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8') at the top of each script that prints non-ASCII. Cleaner but adds boilerplate. Recommend (a) for tests (lowest risk), (b) for pi_agent.py (preserves the existing box-drawing aesthetic where the terminal supports it).
